title: Kepler to lead interoperability tests for ESA’s HydRON optical relay network
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Kepler Communications to lead a hosted payload mission for testing terminal interoperability for its HydRON optical relay network. This initiative aims to advance optical communication capabilities in space.

title: Blue Origin moves toward launch at Vandenberg
summary: Blue Origin is advancing toward launching New Glenn rockets from Vandenberg Space Force Base after the U.S. Space Force agreed to final lease negotiations for Space Launch Complex (SLC)-14. This move aims to enhance the company's heavy and super-heavy space launch capabilities.

title: NRO highlights government and industry partnerships
summary: The National Reconnaissance Office is seeking partnerships with government, industry, academia, and allies to enhance its intelligence and surveillance satellite capabilities. William Adkins, the agency's principal deputy director, emphasized their openness to collaboration to accelerate technological advancements.

title: SES and Boeing move toward factory-installed multi-orbit inflight connectivity
summary: SES and Boeing have agreed to integrate multi-orbit inflight connectivity hardware during aircraft production, shifting from retrofit installations to include LEO broadband. This collaboration marks a significant advancement in in-flight connectivity solutions.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes ispace-U.S. to Board of Directors
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced that ispace-U.S., a U.S.-based lunar exploration company, has joined its Board of Directors. This move highlights the growing collaboration between commercial space entities in advancing lunar exploration.

title: Seagate Space and Oceaneering Join Forces to Build the Future of Offshore Launch Infrastructure
summary: Seagate Space and Oceaneering International have formed a strategic partnership to develop offshore launch platform infrastructure. The collaboration aims to advance Seagate Space's offshore launch platform design through a long-term agreement.

title: Put science back in the driver’s seat
summary: NASA's science missions, which previously led historic discoveries, are now dependent on rides from spacecraft designed for other purposes, reducing their independence. The shift is criticized for potentially limiting scientific advancements in the space industry.

title: The future of GPS satellites with Fang Qian of Lockheed Martin
summary: Fang Qian of Lockheed Martin discusses the future of GPS satellites, focusing on the need for jam-resistant signals. The conversation highlights advancements and challenges in GPS technology.

title: White House releases space nuclear policy
summary: The White House issued a policy on April 14 directing NASA, the Pentagon, and the Department of Energy to develop space nuclear power systems with potential launches by 2028.

title: Defense firms unveil new satellite designs for orbital warfare
summary: BAE Systems and Lockheed Martin are developing new satellite designs capable of maneuvering and being refueled, aimed at enhancing orbital warfare capabilities. These advancements are part of efforts to improve satellite flexibility and operational longevity in space.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moonshot was just the 'opening act' for America's return to the moon, space agency chief says
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission is positioned as the first step in a broader lunar exploration plan, with plans for a moon base and eventual Mars missions. NASA chief Jared Isaacman emphasizes that Artemis 2 is just the beginning of a sustained effort involving international partners.

title: The 10 best Stargate SG-1 episodes, ranked
summary: A list ranking the top 10 episodes of the sci-fi series Stargate SG-1, with the seventh episode titled 'Chevron Seven, Locked!' highlighted as a fan favorite.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon shot just landed on Saturday Night Live — space toilet jokes and all (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission was parodied on Saturday Night Live with space-themed jokes, including references to the spacecraft's toilet. The skit aired the day after the mission's successful splashdown.

title: Scientists use rare 'Einstein Cross' to learn about young galaxy with surprisingly old stars
summary: Scientists utilized a rare 'Einstein Cross' to study a young galaxy with unexpectedly old stars. This discovery provides insights into the early universe's stellar composition.

title: This Artemis 2 astronaut really loves Rise | Space photo of the day for April 14, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 mission features a mascot named 'Rise Wiseman,' which is being celebrated as a beloved figure in space exploration history. The mission is highlighted in a space photo of the day for April 14, 2026.

title: The Lyrids are coming! How I watch meteor showers from the middle of a city
summary: The article discusses how to observe the Lyrid meteor shower from an urban environment using patience, luck, and a good camera setup. It highlights that even city dwellers can witness dazzling fireballs under the right conditions.

title: Artemis 2 calling ISS! Watch the farthest-ever astronaut call from the moon to Earth (video)
summary: Astronauts aboard Artemis 2 and the ISS conducted a historic crew call across the farthest Earth-moon distance ever recorded. This marked the first time such a communication was achieved between the two missions.

title: The moon's oldest and darkest craters could be hiding the most water ice. That's good news for future astronauts
summary: New research indicates that the moon's oldest and darkest craters, particularly those near the south pole in permanent shadow, likely contain the most water ice. This discovery could be beneficial for future lunar missions and astronauts.

title: NASA science faces 'very serious threat' from new White House budget, experts say
summary: NASA's science programs are at risk due to a new White House budget proposal, which experts describe as the least transparent in decades. The cuts threaten critical research and missions across the agency.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4859-4866: One Small Crater and Thousands of Polygons
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover recently drove toward a small crater named 'Antofagasta' after a region in Chile. The rover's activities were detailed in a blog post by the Deputy Project Scientist at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

title: NASA Finds Young Stars Dim in X-rays Surprisingly Quickly
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered that young stars similar to the Sun dim in X-rays faster than expected. The findings, published in The Astrophysical Journal, challenge previous assumptions about stellar activity.

title: NASA Receives 7 Nominations for the 30th Annual Webby Awards
summary: NASA has received seven nominations for the 30th Annual Webby Awards, highlighting its extensive digital communications efforts. The agency's transition from traditional media to social media and online platforms has been acknowledged.

title: 2025-2026 Dream with Us Design Challenge Winners
summary: The 2025-2026 Dream with Us Design Challenge announced its winners, highlighting projects like Scout Farm, AgriTech, and AgriDrone from middle school teams across the U.S.

title: A Hug for Home Away from Home
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch, part of the Artemis II mission, hugs the Orion spacecraft aboard USS John P. Murtha after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean. The crew, including astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, completed their mission.

title: April 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: A puzzle challenges participants to identify the location and significance of a satellite image from April 2026. The image's unique features make it intriguing for analysis.

title: Nutrition Research Arrives Aboard Space Station
summary: NASA's nutrition research arrives at the International Space Station to study plant growth and algae for future long-term missions to the Moon and Mars. This research is crucial for sustaining crew members on distant worlds.

title: NASA’s Webb Redefines Dividing Line Between Planets, Stars
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has helped astronomers examine 29 Cygni b, an object challenging traditional theories of planet formation due to its size. The findings suggest a need to redefine the dividing line between planets and stars.

title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku
summary: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is forecasted to hit the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam in mid-April 2026. This violent storm poses significant risks to the region's infrastructure and safety.

title: NASA Invites Media to Rollout Event for Artemis III Moon Rocket Stage
summary: NASA will roll out the largest section of the SLS rocket for the Artemis III mission from its Michoud Assembly Facility on April 20. This section contains the liquid hydrogen tank and is a key component for the second crewed Artemis mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program. This follows the successful return of Orion, marking a key step in preparation for future missions.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool mission concluding its CRS NG-23 supply run.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II successfully completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II mission is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing efforts to support future missions.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. The launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its midpoint.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to advance lunar exploration.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified topic related to the space industry. No specific details about the news were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. This milestone reflects ESA's contributions to space exploration, science, and technology.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste achieved its first navigation signal, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was highlighted as ideal.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved guidance systems for satellites or probes. This innovation could enhance mission precision and efficiency in space exploration.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, showing significant thrust under microgravity conditions. The study, led by Université Libre de Bruxelles and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating microgravity conditions demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed with versatile capabilities, potentially revolutionizing multiple applications in the space industry. The chip's multifunctional design could enhance efficiency and performance in space missions.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and communicating again.

title: Kepler, Astrolight to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a Kepler Communications-led team, including Astrolight, to test the agency's 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced space-based data transmission capabilities.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the passion and scientific potential of the space industry while questioning the financial planning behind major projects.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: CLD companies challenge NASA's statements, while NASA invites them to disprove their claims. The dispute was highlighted during the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered portal platform to help operators detect anomalies and make decisions. The platform serves as a centralized hub for space operations.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit by ICON, a robotic construction company, with leadership from a former Texas representative. The unit will collaborate with the Pentagon and NASA.

title: Exclusive: Vast Debuts Flight Suit For Haven-1, Private Astronaut Missions
summary: Vast has introduced a new flight suit designed for its Haven-1 space station and upcoming private astronaut missions. The company aims to modernize astronaut apparel with this debut.

title: Atomic-6 Launches Orbital Data Center Marketplace
summary: Atomic-6, a composite manufacturer based in Georgia, has launched ODC.Space, a marketplace to simplify securing orbital compute resources. This initiative aims to accelerate the growth of the orbital data center market.

title: Sophia Space and Kepler Sign Agreement to Demo ODC Tech
summary: Sophia Space and Kepler Communications have signed an agreement to demonstrate orbital-data-center (ODC) technology in space. This marks Sophia Space's first opportunity to validate its ODC software in a real-world orbital environment.

title: Hawkeye 360 Files For IPO
summary: Hawkeye 360, a space-based RF data and analytics firm, has filed for an IPO to raise capital as defense-focused space companies seek public funding. This move aligns with industry growth trends.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. The company aims to leverage this technology to enhance its capabilities in Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO).

title: Rocket Lab Closes Mynaric Acquisition at Higher Price Than Originally Agreed
summary: Rocket Lab completed the acquisition of Mynaric AG for $155.3 million, which is $5.3 million higher than the originally agreed price. The deal focuses on Mynaric's optical communications terminal technology.

title: Allied Space Strategies Stress Complementary Capabilities for Burden Sharing in Orbit
summary: Allied Space Strategies emphasizes the importance of complementary capabilities for burden sharing in space to enhance collective capability, resilience, and deterrence. The discussion highlights how allies and partners can contribute to strengthen shared goals in orbit.

title: NASA Administrator Isaacman Hails the Success of Artemis, Previews Plans for Artemis III and IV
summary: NASA Administrator Isaacman praised the success of the Artemis program and outlined plans for future missions Artemis III and IV. The announcement highlights efforts to accelerate the establishment of a lunar base.

title: Interview With Karman Director, Space & Launch Market Renee Frohnert at Space Symposium
summary: Renee Frohnert of Karman Space & Defense discussed the company's support for Artemis II at the Space Symposium. The interview was conducted by Via Satellite's Managing Editor Rachel Jewett.

title: Delta Air Lines Contracts Airbus to Install Hughes Co-Developed IFC on Upcoming A350-1000 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has contracted Airbus to install a multi-orbit in-flight connectivity (IFC) solution co-developed with Hughes on 20 of its upcoming A350-1000 aircraft. The collaboration aims to enhance passenger connectivity using satellite technology.

title: Amazon Enters D2D Market With Globalstar Acquisition and Plans for its Own System
summary: Amazon has acquired Globalstar to enter the direct-to-device (D2D) satellite market and plans to deploy its own D2D satellite system.

title: EBAD’s Lisa Brown Talks Supporting Customer Missions at Space Symposium
summary: EBAD discussed its long-standing support for critical customer missions, including Artemis II, at the Space Symposium. Lisa Brown highlighted the company's 190-year heritage in the space industry.

title: Lockheed Martin Planning Next-Generation Space Dominance Demos On Orbit
summary: Lockheed Martin is self-funding two next-generation space dominance demonstrations for rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) and command, responding to the U.S. Defense Department's demand for orbital warfare capabilities.

title: Regulators Challenged With Keeping Up With the Cadence of Space Development
summary: Regulators struggle to keep up with the rapid pace of commercial space industry development over the past decade. They face challenges in effectively overseeing and enabling this growth.

title: ThinKom Rolls Out New ThinAir Nexus Multi-Orbit, Multi-System Antenna for Airlines
summary: ThinKom introduced the ThinAir Nexus, a new multi-orbit electronically steered antenna (ESA) for aircraft. The California-based satellite ground-systems company designed the antenna for ease of use.
